title: 250 years of American independence: Thomas Jefferson and John Adams, 'the North and South poles of the American Revolution'
subhead: 'America 250' (4/13). Fifty years to the day after the adoption of the Declaration of Independence, two of its architects died: the enigmatic Thomas Jefferson and the impulsive John Adams. This coincidence only strengthened, among the population, the sense of an unprecedented destiny promised to their nation.

Paris Mayor Grégoire on the climate crisis: 'To adapt together, we need to change our way of life'
subhead: In an interview with Le Monde, the Socialist leader of the French capital city called individual air conditioning 'a scourge' and urged 'flexibility' and 'makeshift solutions' to respond to the urgent challenge of heat waves.

title: 'Impossible to sleep': A night in sweltering France, in search of a cool spot
subhead: With a heatwave bearing down, the French have taken to living after dark, chasing relief from the sun's lingering grip. From Paris to Saint-Malo, by way of Orléans, parks and pools stirred to life as soon as the day ended.

title: Death of Lyhanna: 'Comprehensive law' on Assemblée Nationale's agenda marks victory for feminist and child advocacy groups
subhead: The bill, which aims to strengthen the fight against gender-based and sexual violence, will be debated in the fall, Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu announced after a policy reversal. But a review by the Council of State is expected to reshape the law's scope.

title: Water shortages and heat waves: France on track toward severe summer drought
subhead: Rivers, groundwater tables and soils are under severe strain from insufficient rain and soaring temperatures. The situation has led many departments to set water restrictions, and many fear a challenging summer for water management.

subhead: The war in the Middle East has sped up a realignment that was already underway, forcing the US's partners to rethink flexible alliances without creating overly strong dependencies.

title: Living under Paris's zinc roofs in 40°C heat: 'One day, the people living there will look for those responsible'
subhead: As France faces extreme heat, many of these top-story spaces have become uninhabitable. Some residents have formed groups to call on their landlords, neighbors and local authorities to address their living conditions.

title: France faces unprecedented heatwave: 'We are about to experience four of the hottest days ever recorded in the country'
subhead: A prolonged heatwave, rather than a brief spike, has arrived. Unusually high temperatures are set to persist across the country until at least the end of this week, exposing more than 90% of the French population to extreme and rare heat.
